food_subgroup: Stir-fried wheat-egg noodles with pork, liver, shrimp, and vegetables

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists specifically for Pancit Canton. FOODON_03315674 (noodle dish) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists specifically for Pancit Canton. A0CMF (stir-fried noodles) is the closest defensible parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Pancit Canton or stir-fried noodle dish category.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of Pancit Canton weighs approximately 350g and provides roughly 490 kcal, 28g protein, 14g fat, 60g carbohydrate, and approximately 950mg sodium.

nutrient_source.title: Philippine Food Composition Tables (PFCT) — Pancit Canton, cooked composite dish
nutrient_source.organization: Food and Nutrition Research Institute (FNRI), Department of Science and Technology (DOST), Philippines
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are taken from the FNRI-DOST Philippine Food Composition Tables (PFCT). The PFCT is listed under the FAO/INFOODS SEA regional food composition network and is an approved primary regional source under Project Veritas source policy (Section 5.1, item 4: FAO/INFOODS SEA — Southeast Asian dishes). Values represent the standard cooked Pancit Canton composite dish (pork, liver, shrimp, vegetables) per 100g edible portion as reported in the PFCT. No adaptation or estimation was required.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Egg Noodles and Wheat-Based Noodle Dishes — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database — peer-reviewed GI tables (Diabetes Care, 2008)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of ~58 is an analogous estimate derived from the University of Sydney GI Database and the International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values (Atkinson, Foster-Powell, Brand-Miller, Diabetes Care 2008), which are approved GI sources under Project Veritas source policy (Section 5.1, item 8). The closest analogous entry is cooked egg noodles / wheat-based noodles (GI range 46–61, midpoint ~54–58). Canton noodles are egg-wheat noodles; the egg protein matrix and wheat gluten network lower the GI relative to rice noodles (GI ~61–72). GI of 58 is the upper-midpoint estimate, consistent with a moderate-GI classification. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (58 × 60) / 100 = 34.8. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~950mg per 350g plate; ~271mg per 100g) from soy sauce, oyster sauce, and fish sauce
hypertension.guidance: Hypertensive patients should limit Pancit Canton to a half-plate portion (~175g, ~475mg sodium) or request reduced soy sauce; using low-sodium soy sauce at home can reduce sodium by approximately 40%, and the dish cannot be de-salted after cooking unlike clear soups.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate GI (~58) and high GL (~34.8 per plate) partially offset by high protein (28g per plate) and vegetable fibre (~4.5g per plate)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should consume a half-plate portion (~175g) to limit glycemic load to approximately 17, pair with additional non-starchy vegetables, and monitor postprandial glucose given the substantial carbohydrate load of 60g per full plate.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium (~950mg per plate) and moderate cholesterol (~193mg per plate from liver and shrimp); saturated fat is relatively low at ~4.2g per plate
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Cardiovascular disease patients should limit portion size to reduce sodium and cholesterol exposure; the chicken liver component provides beneficial B12 and iron but also contributes dietary cholesterol, so frequency should be moderated to 1–2 servings per week within a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Chicken liver provides exceptional vitamin B12 (~2.8mcg per 100g) and iron (~1.8mg per 100g), critical for RA patients on methotrexate; wheat noodles contain gluten relevant for RA patients with concurrent coeliac disease
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: RA patients on methotrexate benefit from the B12 and folate in chicken liver; however, the high sodium (~950mg per plate) requires monitoring for corticosteroid-related fluid retention, and gluten-sensitive RA patients should substitute rice noodles.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pancit Canton is the Philippines' most celebrated noodle dish — stir-fried egg-wheat noodles (canton noodles) with pork strips, chicken liver, shrimp, Chinese cabbage (pechay), snap peas, and carrots in a soy-oyster sauce. The dish has Chinese-Hokkien origins, introduced to the Philippines in the 16th–17th century, and is ubiquitous at Filipino birthday celebrations and fiestas, symbolising long life. Per standard plate (~350g): approximately 490 kcal, 28g protein, 14g fat, 60g carbohydrate, GI ~58 (medium), GL ~34.8, and ~950mg sodium. The egg noodles produce a lower GI than rice noodles due to the egg protein matrix. The chicken liver component contributes exceptional vitamin B12 (~2.8mcg per 100g), iron (~1.8mg per 100g), and vitamin A (~80mcg RAE per 100g). Texture is tender-chewy noodles with crisp-tender vegetables and savoury umami sauce. Clinically, the high sodium content (~271mg per 100g) is the primary concern for hypertensive and cardiovascular patients. The dish is dairy-free and widely available at Filipino restaurants, Jollibee outlets in Singapore, and at Filipino community celebrations. Source: FNRI-DOST Philippine Food Composition Tables (PFCT), an approved FAO/INFOODS SEA regional source.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the FNRI-DOST Philippine Food Composition Tables (PFCT), which is an approved primary regional source under the FAO/INFOODS SEA network for Southeast Asian dishes. GI is a defensible analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and the peer-reviewed International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008), both approved GI sources. All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are fully populated from the PFCT source.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for Pancit Canton not available in FNRI-DOST PFCT as of 2026-04-14. water_g set to -1: moisture content for the composite cooked dish not reported in FNRI-DOST PFCT as of 2026-04-14. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per FNRI-DOST PFCT — no hydrogenated fats used in traditional preparation). GI of 58 is an analogous estimate based on cooked egg-wheat noodles from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (Diabetes Care 2008); no direct GI measurement for Pancit Canton exists in the published literature as of 2026-04-14. Vitamin A reported as mcg RAE (retinol activity equivalents) — the chicken liver component is the primary contributor. Sodium per plate (~950mg) calculated as 271mg/100g × 3.5 (350g serving). GL per serving = (58 × 60) / 100 = 34.8. Filipino cuisine is not a Singaporean dish category; multilingual requirements for Singaporean dishes (Tamil, Hindi) do not apply — Chinese and Malay aliases are provided for regional relevance in the Singapore Filipino diaspora context. Nutrient source FNRI-DOST PFCT is an approved source under Project Veritas source policy Section 5.1 item 4 (FAO/INFOODS SEA — Southeast Asian dishes).
